spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates CSS Color 5 alpha() function deriving alpha channel modifications directly in CSS.

interactive.rationale: The token swatches are static; adding color pickers to dynamically update the base tokens would make the interactivity meaningful.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The blurb accurately describes how alpha() simplifies design token systems by deriving alpha variants dynamically.

edge_cases.rationale: Lacks CSS @supports fallbacks or visual indicators for browsers that do not support the alpha() function.

references.rationale: Cites the correct ChromeStatus reference link in the 'see also' section.

design_system.rationale: Adheres to the design system, utilizing standard CSS variables and maintaining good contrast.

openQuestions.title: Add interactive color pickers for design tokens
openQuestions.detail: Allow users to change the base --brand, --success, --danger, and --neutral colors using color inputs to see how the derived alpha() swatches and alert banners update live.

openQuestions.title: Add fallback styles for unsupported browsers
openQuestions.detail: Use @supports to provide a fallback color so the swatches do not disappear in non-supporting browsers, and display a warning message.
